description:
Adds a "Send SMS Manually" button to the hr.applicant Action menu
(form + list) and the SMS preview popup. Clicking it opens a modal
showing:
  - Applicant name (heading)
  - Static HR-phone-only warning (always visible — reminds HR the
    system never sends SMS, it must be sent from the HR phone)
  - Mobile number — rendered as HTML. If the number is missing the
    cell reads "No Phone number" in red; if present but fewer than
    10 digits the number itself is shown in red. Otherwise plain text.
  - Pre-filled SMS text matching the applicant's current stage
...and two action buttons in the footer:
  - SMS Sent → stamps the right x_studio_sms_*_date field, clears
    the preview and warning fields, posts chatter audit, closes
  - Cancel → closes without any state change

Stage routing (same wording as the SMS Queue Digest in 002):
  - Stage 1 (New) with no attachment → resume-missing SMS, stamps
    x_studio_sms_new_reminder_date
  - Stage 2 (Qualification) → info-survey SMS, stamps
    x_studio_sms_reminder_date
  - Stage 7 (Assessment Sent) → assessment SMS, stamps
    x_studio_assessment_sms_reminder_date
  - Any other stage → UserError ("no SMS template for this stage")

Implementation: a custom form view of hr.applicant rendered in
target='new' modal mode. Two scratch fields on hr.applicant hold the
rendered SMS text and the phone-validity warning between click and
modal render. Pure procedural Python — no closures, no hasattr.
